steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a modifier placement (dangling modifier) question. The introductory phrase "Upon recovering two years later" must modify the subject of the main clause. That subject must be Henry — he is the one who recovered — and the main clause must then logically connect to forcing Richard from court.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Henry resumed his reign,": Places "Henry" immediately after the introductory phrase, so it correctly modifies him. Henry (a person) logically recovered and then forced Richard from court. ✓
  • "the reign of Henry resumed,": Dangling modifier — "the reign of Henry" becomes the subject, illogically implying that Henry's reign (not Henry himself) recovered from illness. ✗
  • "Henry's reign resumed,": Also a dangling modifier — "Henry's reign" is the subject, again suggesting the reign recovered from illness rather than Henry. ✗
  • "it was Henry who resumed his reign,": A cleft construction that places "it" as the grammatical subject immediately after the phrase, creating a dangling modifier: "it" illogically appears to be what recovered two years later. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
